name: CRUD Operations
description: Generic database CRUD abstraction layer for Supabase with filtering, sorting, pagination, and relational query support.

intent.what: Provides clean, type-safe functions for all standard database operations: create, read, update, delete. Supports composable filters, sorting, pagination, and validation.
intent.why: Eliminates repetitive database code, ensures consistent error handling, and provides a Prisma-like developer experience on top of Supabase.

steps: Import the desired function from nexora-engine
Call with table name and options
code_examples: const { data } = await fetchAll<User>('users', { filters: [{ column: 'status', operator: 'eq', value: 'active' }] })

internal_flow: Execute with timeout and retry support

execution_reasoning.query_execution: All queries go through executeRequest() for consistent timeout/retry
execution_reasoning.cache: Cache integration happens at QueryEngine layer
execution_reasoning.filters: Map directly to Supabase query builders

rules: Table name must be a valid Supabase table
ID must be a string or number
Filter operators must be valid Supabase operators
anti_patterns: Don't forget to handle null data
Don't use any type - use proper generics

when_to_use: Use fetchAll for listing records with optional filters
Use fetchById when you know the primary key
Use fetchPaginated for paginated list views
Use mutation functions for write operations
reasoning_strategy: Choose appropriate function based on needs
